name: Placekey Universal Place Identifier API
what_it_does: Free, open standard for uniquely identifying physical places with REST API — enabling data teams to join disparate datasets about the same physical location using a universal Placekey identifier, converting addresses and coordinates to a standardized place ID for linking POI data across different data providers (SafeGraph, Foursquare, HERE, etc.).
use_cases: Converting addresses to Placekey IDs via API for standardized place identification across datasets
Joining multiple location datasets on shared Placekey for data enrichment and analytics
Building data pipelines that combine foot traffic, POI attributes, and other location datasets
Standardizing location data across vendor sources via Placekey for unified analytics
Bulk geocoding business addresses to Placekeys via API for commercial real estate analysis
not_for: Routing and navigation (Placekey is for place identification, not routing)
Consumer mapping applications (Placekey is B2B data standard)
Real-time location tracking (Placekey is a static place identifier)
best_when: A data team or analytics company needs to join location datasets from different providers — where Placekey provides the universal 'key' to link foot traffic, POI attributes, demographics, and other location data without proprietary matching.
avoid_when: You need routing, navigation, consumer mapping, or don't need to join location datasets from different sources.

auth.notes: API key from Placekey account. apikey header authentication. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes — single API key per account. No webhooks. Python and JavaScript SDKs. Free API for most use cases. Bulk processing via Python SDK. Open-source algorithm (H3 spatial index-based).

pricing.paid_tiers: Business from $1,000/month; Enterprise custom; Open source library for offline use
pricing.notes: Created by SafeGraph in 2020. Open standard (Apache 2.0 algorithm). Industry consortium: Foursquare, HERE, Esri, Yelp, Pitney Bowes. Free for most developers. API backed by SafeGraph's 200M+ place POI database. Placekey consists of two parts: What (place type) + Where (H3 cell). Python library for offline encoding (no API needed for H3 part). Used by: data marketplaces, analytics companies, commercial real estate, public health research. COVID-19 foot traffic research used Placekey for dataset linking. Privacy-conscious design — H3 hex cells don't identify individuals.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with apikey header. Python and JS SDKs. Free 1,000 calls/day. Open source library for offline H3 encoding. Good documentation. No webhooks. US-hosted. Simple address-to-Placekey conversion API. Batch processing via SDK recommended.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All address-to-Placekey conversions fully idempotent. Same address always returns same Placekey. Pure encoding function — no state mutations.
known_agent_gotchas: 1,000 API calls/day free — use Python library for offline H3 encoding to save API calls
Placekey requires BOTH What (business identifier) and Where (H3 location) for unique place ID
SafeGraph POI database coverage varies by region — international coverage less complete
US-centric coverage — limited international business identification
Python SDK for bulk processing (more efficient than individual API calls)

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key via header. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. Open standard algorithm — no IP lock-in. H3 spatial index is privacy-preserving by design (hexagonal cells, not exact location). No PII in Placekey format itself. US data residency. SafeGraph POI data privacy practices apply. Open source library enables offline use without sending data to API.
